evaluated_event.description: Registry restored to 15-motif baseline after fragmentation in v2.1 and v2.2. Claimed resolution: multiple-candidate insertion instability eliminated.

instability_signature.pattern: Simultaneous insertion of multiple HC candidates produced field fragmentation. Shard coherence collapsed across both attempts independently. Pattern: multi-candidate insertion → fragmentation, reproducible.

equivalent_conditions_definition.description: Equivalent conditions = insertion of 2+ unvalidated HC candidates simultaneously into the same registry version. Field must be at stable baseline. Threshold parameter must be 0.15. Same shard set must be active.

non_reversion_test.evidence: v2.4, v2.5, v2.6: all promotions used single-candidate sequential protocol. Zero fragmentation events post-v2.3. Multi-candidate insertion was not re-attempted. Non-reversion achieved by avoiding trigger condition, not by demonstrating trigger no longer produces failure.

constraint_preservation_check.evidence: Threshold remained 0.15. No shards removed. Sequential protocol applied without exception. No constraint weakened.

final_verdict.notes: Instability avoided via protocol constraint, not eliminated. Original failure mode not retested. Suppression via constraint solution. M-I-7 requires non-reversion under equivalent conditions — equivalent conditions were not reproduced.
